services.name: EC2
services.description: Resizable compute capacity in the cloud. Launch virtual servers, configure networking and security, and manage storage.
keyPoints: Instance types: General (T3/M5), Compute (C5), Memory (R5), Storage (I3), Accelerated (P3/G4)
Pricing: On-Demand, Reserved (1-3yr), Spot (up to 90% off), Savings Plans
AMIs: Amazon Machine Images — pre-configured templates for launches
Auto Scaling groups for horizontal elasticity
Instance metadata available at 169.254.169.254
examTips: Know the difference between horizontal scaling (more instances) and vertical scaling (bigger instance)
Spot instances can save up to 90% but can be interrupted with 2-min warning
Placement groups: Cluster (low latency), Spread (max availability), Partition (large distributed workloads)
Reserved Instances can be Standard (modify AZ) or Convertible (change instance family)

quiz.q: Which EC2 pricing model offers the largest discount for steady-state workloads?
quiz.explanation: Reserved Instances offer up to 72% discount for 1-3 year commitments on steady-state workloads. Spot is cheaper but can be interrupted.

quiz.q: Which placement group type provides the lowest network latency between instances?
quiz.explanation: Cluster placement groups pack instances close together in a single AZ for the lowest latency (10 Gbps network).

services.name: Lambda
services.tagline: Run code without thinking about servers
services.description: Serverless compute service that runs your code in response to events. You pay only for the compute time consumed — no charge when code is not running.
keyPoints: Event-driven: triggered by S3, DynamoDB, API Gateway, SNS, SQS, etc.
Max execution time: 15 minutes per invocation
Memory: 128 MB to 10,240 MB (CPU scales with memory)
Supports Python, Node.js, Java, Go, .NET, Ruby, custom runtimes
Concurrency: default 1000 per region, can request increase
examTips: Lambda@Edge runs at CloudFront edge locations (5-second timeout)
Cold starts: first invocation is slower — use Provisioned Concurrency to eliminate
Lambda layers let you share code/libraries across functions
Invocation types: Synchronous, Asynchronous, Event source mapping

quiz.q: What is the maximum execution timeout for a single Lambda invocation?
quiz.explanation: Lambda functions can run for a maximum of 15 minutes (900 seconds) per invocation.

quiz.q: Which feature eliminates Lambda cold starts?
quiz.explanation: Provisioned Concurrency keeps functions initialized and ready to respond in milliseconds, eliminating cold starts.

services.name: ECS
services.description: Fully managed container orchestration service. Run, stop, and manage Docker containers on a cluster of EC2 instances or with Fargate.
keyPoints: Task Definitions: blueprints describing containers (image, CPU, memory, ports)
Services: maintain desired count of tasks, integrate with ELB
Launch types: EC2 (you manage instances) or Fargate (serverless)
Integrates with ECR (Elastic Container Registry) for images
Service Discovery via AWS Cloud Map
examTips: ECS + EC2: you manage the cluster capacity and patching
ECS + Fargate: AWS manages infrastructure — you just define tasks
Task IAM roles provide per-task permissions (not per-instance)
ECS Anywhere extends ECS to on-premises infrastructure

quiz.q: Which ECS launch type removes the need to manage underlying EC2 instances?
quiz.explanation: Fargate is the serverless launch type for ECS — AWS manages the compute infrastructure, you just define your containers.

services.name: Fargate
services.description: Serverless compute engine for containers that works with ECS and EKS. No need to provision or manage servers.
keyPoints: Pay per vCPU and memory per second (minimum 1 minute)
No EC2 instances to manage, patch, or scale
Each task gets its own isolation boundary (no sharing kernel)
Works with both ECS and EKS
Supports ephemeral storage up to 200 GB
examTips: Fargate is more expensive per unit than EC2 but eliminates management overhead
Use Fargate Spot for fault-tolerant workloads (up to 70% off)
Fargate tasks can mount EFS filesystems for persistent storage
No SSH access to underlying hosts — use ECS Exec for debugging

quiz.q: Which statement about Fargate is FALSE?
options: It works with both ECS and EKS
You can SSH into the underlying host
Each task has its own isolation boundary
quiz.explanation: You cannot SSH into Fargate hosts — there are no visible instances. Use ECS Exec for container debugging.

services.name: Elastic Beanstalk
services.tagline: Deploy web apps without managing infrastructure
services.description: PaaS that handles deployment, capacity provisioning, load balancing, auto-scaling, and health monitoring. You upload code, Beanstalk handles the rest.
keyPoints: Supports Java, .NET, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, Docker
Manages EC2, ELB, Auto Scaling, RDS automatically
Environment tiers: Web Server (HTTP) and Worker (background SQS)
Deployment policies: All at once, Rolling, Rolling with batch, Immutable, Blue/Green
You retain full control over underlying resources
examTips: Beanstalk is free — you only pay for underlying resources
Immutable deployment creates a new ASG for zero-downtime deploys
Blue/Green: swap environment URLs for instant cutover
.ebextensions/ folder for custom configuration
Beanstalk != serverless — it still uses EC2 instances

quiz.q: Which Elastic Beanstalk deployment policy creates an entirely new set of instances?
quiz.explanation: Immutable deployment launches a fresh set of instances in a new Auto Scaling group, ensuring zero-downtime and easy rollback.

services.name: Batch
services.tagline: Fully managed batch processing at scale
services.description: Efficiently run hundreds of thousands of batch computing jobs. Dynamically provisions the optimal quantity and type of compute resources.
keyPoints: Job Definitions: specify Docker image, vCPU, memory, env vars
Job Queues: prioritized queues that map to compute environments
Compute Environments: Managed (AWS provisions) or Unmanaged (you manage)
Supports Spot Instances for cost optimization
Integrates with Step Functions for complex workflows
examTips: Batch on Fargate: no EC2 management, faster startup for small jobs
Array jobs: run multiple copies with different parameters
Batch handles job scheduling, retries, and dependencies
Multi-node parallel jobs for tightly coupled HPC workloads

quiz.q: What does AWS Batch automatically manage for you?
quiz.explanation: AWS Batch automatically provisions the right amount and type of compute resources, schedules jobs, and manages dependencies.

services.name: Lightsail
services.description: Easy-to-use VPS with a simple management interface. Includes compute, storage, and networking in a predictable monthly price.
keyPoints: Fixed monthly pricing: includes compute, storage, data transfer
Pre-configured blueprints: WordPress, LAMP, Node.js, etc.
Managed databases, load balancers, and CDN distributions
Automatic snapshots for backup
Can export to EC2 when you outgrow Lightsail
examTips: Lightsail is designed for simplicity — when workloads get complex, migrate to EC2
Predictable billing: no surprise charges for included data transfer
Cannot use Lightsail instances in Auto Scaling groups
Lightsail containers available for simple containerized apps

quiz.q: When should you consider migrating from Lightsail to EC2?
options: When you need a simple website
When workloads require advanced networking or scaling
quiz.explanation: Lightsail is for simple workloads. Migrate to EC2 when you need Auto Scaling, advanced VPC features, or more instance type choices.
